questionText: Break down your total gross global Scope 1 emissions from oil and gas value chain production activities by greenhouse gas type.
questionKo: your total 전 세계 총 Scope 1 배출량 from oil 및 gas 가치사슬 production activities by greenhouse gas type.을/를 세분화하여 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Reporting gross global Scope 1 emissions by emission category allows for a more in-depth understanding of business risks, such as exposure to future regulation. The emissions categories are broken down to provide data users with a relevant and complete understanding of your organization's oil and gas production activities and how these contribute to your emissions profile.

columns.text: Gross Scope 1 CO2 emissions (metric tons CO2)
Gross Scope 1 methane emissions (metric tons CH4)
columns.textKo: Gross Scope 1 methane emissions (metric tons CH4)

columns.text: Total gross Scope 1 emissions (metric tons CO2e)

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes, both Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions" or "Yes, Scope 1 emissions only" in response to column 1 "Emissions breakdown by greenhouse gas type" of 7.15.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes, both Scope 1 및 Scope 2 배출량" 또는 "Yes, Scope 1 배출량 only" in response to 열 1 "Emissions breakdown by greenhouse gas type" of 7.15.

guidance.text:
General 
This question requests emissions data only from emission sources that fall within the chosen reporting boundary (i.e. Scope 1 emissions) and that are within the oil & gas sector boundary.
Negative numbers are not allowed as organizations are to report gross, not net figures.
Emissions figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4).
Emissions category (column 1) 
Select the emissions category or categories which apply to the emissions breakdown being reported.
If you are unable to disaggregate your emissions as requested then you have the option to provide other categories using the "Other, please specify" option. Equally, if you wish to disaggregate further (e.g. to include combustion emissions from captured streams) then you may do so here.
Value chain (column 2) 
Select the value chain or parts of the value chain which apply to the emissions breakdown being reported.
If the breakdown of emissions does not apply to the value chains listed then select the option "Other, please specify" and outline the part of the oil and gas value chain that the breakdown does apply to.
Product (column 3) 
Select the product that the emissions breakdown applies to, if you cannot disaggregate the figure by oil or gas then select the option "Unable to disaggregate".
Gross Scope 1 CO2 emissions (metric tons CO2) (column 4) 
Report your organization's Scope 1 CO2 emissions in metric tons CO2 for the emissions category in column 1.
Gross Scope 1 methane emissions (metric tons CH4) (column 5) 
Report your organization's Scope 1 methane emissions in metric tons CH4 for the emissions category in column 1.
Gross Scope 1 emissions (metric tons CO2e) (column 6) 
Total greenhouse gas emissions should be aggregated and reported in units of CO2e.
Greenhouse gas emissions include all gasses identified in the Kyoto Protocol, therefore the figure provided in this column may be higher than the sum of CO2 and CH4 (provided in columns 4 and 5 respectively).
The total of GHG emissions (the sum of all rows in column 6) from this question's table should be equal to the figures reported in question 7.19 (oil and gas production activities upstream and oil and gas production activities downstream).
The Global Warming Potential (GWP) factors used here should be consisted with your disclosures throughout the questionnaire. CDP encourages the use of the most recent GWP factors published by IPCC, in alignment with the GHG Protocol .
Note on oil and gas sector boundary 
As a production sector, CDP describes the activities of the oil and gas sector as "oil and gas production activities". These activities may be directly or indirectly related to the production process itself. Given the potential complexity of production sectors, CDP encourages you to identify and remove specific activities from your organizational boundary (or oil and gas business division's organizational boundary) that are not necessarily a part of the oil and gas sector. Starting with your answer to question 7.6, emissions from the following sources should be deducted:
External corporate entities, i.e. assets, business divisions, partnerships and subsidiaries operating outside of the oil and gas sector (or value chain).
Non-industrial buildings, e.g. offices, accommodation, other property.
Non-production related activities, e.g. management, services, R&D, marketing, retail.
Transport*, e.g. distribution, business travel, shipping, freight, logistics.
Projects**, e.g. construction, engineering and maintenance.
Alternatively, you may consider constructing your sector boundary around activities that should be included . At a 
minimum , you should include in your sector boundary:
The production processes.
All activities, processes and equipment that are ancillary to said production processes.
All other industrial installations, energy installations and other installations or activities contributing to or supplying said production processes and ancillary activities, e.g. boilers, power plant, raw material preparation and extraction, etc.
All buildings that house said production processes and ancillary activities and said installations, as well as buildings used for inventory storage.
Onsite mobile combustion, e.g. forklifts and excavators, and movement of materials between industrial sites within the sector.
Any other industrial activities that typically occur on the production sites of the oil and gas sector.
* Transport, storage, and distribution activities considered a defining part of the oil and gas sector value chain, e.g. midstream activities, should be included for oil and gas production activities.
** Projects considered an essential part of the oil and gas sector, such as exploration and extraction projects, should be included for oil and gas production activities.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 requests 배출량 데이터 only from emission sources that fall within the chosen 보고경계 (i.e. Scope 1 배출량) 및 that are within the oil & gas sector boundary. Negative numbers are not allowed as organizations are to report gross, not net figures. Emissions figures 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). Emissions category (열 1) Select the emissions category 또는 categories which apply to the emissions breakdown being 보고. 만약 you are unable to disaggregate your emissions as requested then you have the option to provide other categories using the "Other, 직접 입력" option. Equally, 만약 you wish to disaggregate further (e.g. to include combustion emissions from captured streams) then you may do so here. 가치사슬 (열 2) Select the 가치사슬 또는 parts 의 가치사슬 which apply to the emissions breakdown being 보고. 만약 the breakdown of emissions does not apply to the 가치사슬s listed then select the option "Other, 직접 입력" 및 outline the part 의 석유 및 가스 가치사슬 that the breakdown does apply to. Product (열 3) Select the product that the emissions breakdown applies to, 만약 you cannot disaggregate the figure by oil 또는 gas then select the option "Unable to disaggregate". Gross Scope 1 CO2 emissions (미터톤 CO2) (열 4) Report 귀사의 Scope 1 CO2 emissions in 미터톤 CO2 에 대한 emissions category in 열 1. Gross Scope 1 methane emissions (metric tons CH4) (열 5) Report 귀사의 Scope 1 methane emissions in metric tons CH4 에 대한 emissions category in 열 1. Gross Scope 1 배출량 (미터톤 CO2e) (열 6) Total 온실가스 배출량 해야 합니다 be aggregated 및 보고 in units of CO2e. 온실가스 배출량 include all gasses identified in the Kyoto Protocol, therefore the figure 입력 in this 열 may be higher than the sum of CO2 및 CH4 (입력 in 열 4 및 5 respectively). The total of 온실가스 배출량 (the sum of all 행 in 열 6) from this 문항's table 해야 합니다 be equal to the figures 보고 in 문항 7.19 (oil 및 gas production activities upstream 및 oil 및 gas production activities downstream). The Global Warming Potential (GWP) factors used here 해야 합니다 be consisted with your 공시s throughout the 질문서. CDP encourages the use 의 most recent GWP factors published by IPCC, in alignment with the GHG Protocol. Note on oil 및 gas sector boundary As a production sector, CDP 설명s the activities 의 oil 및 gas sector as "oil 및 gas production activities". These activities may be directly 또는 indirectly related to the production 프로세스 itself. Given the potential complexity of production sectors, CDP encourages you to identify 및 remove specific activities from your 조직경계 (or oil 및 gas business division's 조직경계) that are not necessarily a part 의 oil 및 gas sector. Starting with your answer to 문항 7.6, emissions from the following sources 해야 합니다 be deducted: External corporate entities, i.e. 자산, business divisions, partnerships 및 subsidiaries operating outside 의 oil 및 gas sector (or 가치사슬). Non-industrial buildings, e.g. offices, accommodation, other property. Non-production related activities, e.g. 관리, services, R&D, marketing, retail. Transport*, e.g. distribution, business travel, shipping, freight, logistics. Projects**, e.g. construction, engineering 및 maintenance. Alternatively, you may consider constructing your sector boundary around activities that 해야 합니다 be included. At a 최소, you 해야 합니다 include in your sector boundary: The production 프로세스es. All activities, 프로세스es 및 equipment that are ancillary to said production 프로세스es. All other industrial installations, energy installations 및 other installations 또는 activities contributing to 또는 supplying said production 프로세스es 및 ancillary activities, e.g. boilers, power plant, raw material preparation 및 extraction, etc. All buildings that house said production 프로세스es 및 ancillary activities 및 said installations, as well as buildings used for inventory storage. Onsite mobile combustion, e.g. forklifts 및 excavators, 및 movement of materials between industrial sites within the sector. 하나 이상 other industrial activities that typically occur on the production sites 의 oil 및 gas sector. * Transport, storage, 및 distribution activities considered a defining part 의 oil 및 gas sector 가치사슬, e.g. midstream activities, 해야 합니다 be included for oil 및 gas production activities. ** Projects considered an essential part 의 oil 및 gas sector, such as exploration 및 extraction projects, 해야 합니다 be included for oil 및 gas production activities.

guidance.text:
Combustion: Combustion refers to combustion within the company's boundary giving rise to emissions of CO2, N2O, and CH4. Sources may include boilers, heaters, furnaces, incinerators, internal combustion engines, and turbines. Scope 1 GHG emissions excludes emission of CO2 arising from the combustion and fermentation of biomass and biofuels; these emissions are reported as a separate category.
Flaring: Includes emissions from elevated flares, ground flares, emergency flares, well-testing and well work-over.
Fugitives: This includes emissions from unintentional leaks (or system malfunctions) from sources such as valves, fittings, flanges, compressor seals, other compressor related leaks, heaters, dehydrators, and pipelines. Accidental fugitive emissions can be individually found and fixed in order to make the emissions near zero. Emissions from non-point sources, such as wastewater treatment and surface impoundments, should be accounted for under fugitive emissions.
Process emissions: Emissions from industrial production processes which chemically or physically transform materials (e.g. CO2 from the calcinations step in cement manufacturing, CO2 from catalytic cracking in petrochemical processing, PFC emissions from aluminum smelting, etc.).
Venting: Emissions from venting of associated gas and waste gas/vapor streams at oil and gas facilities. Vented emissions or intentional processing venting, arise from process, maintenance, turnarounds, non-routine activities and other activities, and include emissions of CH4 and CO2 occurring from such sources as inoperative flares, flashing of gas in crude oil or condensate storage tanks, pneumatic devices driven by natural gas, starters, pressure relief valves, blowdowns (vessels, pipelines, and compressors), compressor seals, pumps, loading operations, shipping operations, venting and purging, exploration and well testing, venting of casinghead gas from oil wells, maintenance and turnaround, and non-routine releases.
guidance.textKo: Combustion: Combustion refers to combustion within the company's boundary giving rise to emissions of CO2, N2O, 및 CH4. Sources may include boilers, heaters, furnaces, incinerators, internal combustion engines, 및 turbines. Scope 1 온실가스 배출량 excludes emission of CO2 arising from the combustion 및 fermentation of biomass 및 biofuels; these emissions are 보고 as a separate category. Flaring: Includes emissions from elevated flares, ground flares, emergency flares, well-testing 및 well work-over. Fugitives: This includes emissions from unintentional leaks (or system malfunctions) from sources such as valves, fittings, flanges, compressor seals, other compressor related leaks, heaters, dehydrators, 및 pipelines. Accidental fugitive emissions can be individually found 및 fixed in order to make the emissions near zero. Emissions from non-점 sources, such as wastewater treatment 및 surface impoundments, 해야 합니다 be accounted for under fugitive emissions. 프로세스 emissions: Emissions from industrial production 프로세스es which chemically 또는 physically transform materials (e.g. CO2 from the calcinations step in cement manufacturing, CO2 from catalytic cracking in petrochemical 프로세스ing, PFC emissions from aluminum smelting, etc.). Venting: Emissions from venting of associated gas 및 waste gas/vapor streams at oil 및 gas facilities. Vented emissions 또는 intentional 프로세스ing venting, arise from 프로세스, maintenance, turnarounds, non-routine activities 및 other activities, 및 include emissions of CH4 및 CO2 occurring from such sources as inoperative flares, flashing of gas in crude oil 또는 condensate storage tanks, pneumatic devices driven by natural gas, starters, pressure relief valves, blowdowns (vessels, pipelines, 및 compressors), compressor seals, pumps, loading operations, shipping operations, venting 및 purging, exploration 및 well testing, venting of casinghead gas from oil wells, maintenance 및 turnaround, 및 non-routine releases.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 6/6 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 6/6점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed.
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성 행 in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: Reporting 전 세계 총 Scope 1 배출량 by emission category allows for a more in-depth understanding of business 리스크, such as exposure to future regulation. The emissions categories are broken down to provide 데이터 사용자 with a 관련 및 complete understanding of 귀사의 oil 및 gas production activities 및 how these contribute to your emissions profile.
